name: api-and-interface-design
description_raw: Guides stable API and interface design. Use when designing APIs, module boundaries, or any public interface. Use when creating REST or GraphQL endpoints, defining type contracts between modules, or establishing boundaries between frontend and backend.
description_normalized: Guides stable, hard-to-misuse API and interface design across REST, GraphQL, module boundaries, and component contracts.

frontmatter_yaml.name: api-and-interface-design
frontmatter_yaml.description: Guides stable API and interface design. Use when designing APIs, module boundaries, or any public interface. Use when creating REST or GraphQL endpoints, defining type contracts between modules, or establishing boundaries between frontend and backend.

name: idea-refine
description_raw: Refines raw ideas into sharp, actionable concepts through structured divergent and convergent thinking. Use when an idea is still vague, when you need to stress-test assumptions before committing to a plan, or when you want to expand options before converging on one. Triggers on "ideate", "refine this idea", or "stress-test my plan".
description_normalized: Turns vague ideas into sharp, actionable concepts via structured divergent (expand) then convergent (stress-test, converge) thinking.

frontmatter_yaml.name: idea-refine
frontmatter_yaml.description: Refines raw ideas into sharp, actionable concepts through structured divergent and convergent thinking. Use when an idea is still vague, when you need to stress-test assumptions before committing to a plan, or when you want to expand options before converging on one. Triggers on "ideate", "refine this idea", or "stress-test my plan".

name: interview-me
description_raw: Extracts what the user actually wants instead of what they think they should want. Achieves this through one-question-at-a-time interview until ~95% confidence about the underlying intent. Use when an ask is underspecified, when the user explicitly invokes, or when you catch yourself silently filling in ambiguous requirements before any plan, spec, or code exists.
description_normalized: Closes the gap between what users ask for and what they actually want via one-question-at-a-time interview before any plan or code begins.

frontmatter_yaml.name: interview-me
frontmatter_yaml.description: Extracts what the user actually wants instead of what they think they should want. Achieves this through one-question-at-a-time interview until ~95% confidence about the underlying intent. Use when an ask is underspecified ("build me X" without "for whom" or "why now"), when the user explicitly invokes ("interview me", "grill me", "are we sure?", "stress-test my thinking"), or when you catch yourself silently filling in ambiguous requirements before any plan, spec, or code exists.

name: spec-driven-development
description_raw: Creates specs before coding. Use when starting a new project, feature, or significant change and no specification exists yet. Use when requirements are unclear, ambiguous, or only exist as a vague idea.
description_normalized: Writes a structured specification as shared source of truth before any code is written, treating unspecced code as guessing.

frontmatter_yaml.name: spec-driven-development
frontmatter_yaml.description: Creates specs before coding. Use when starting a new project, feature, or significant change and no specification exists yet. Use when requirements are unclear, ambiguous, or only exist as a vague idea.

name: spec
description_raw: Start spec-driven development — write a structured specification before writing code
description_normalized: Orchestration entry point that invokes the spec-driven-development skill to write a structured specification before any code begins.

frontmatter_yaml.description: Start spec-driven development — write a structured specification before writing code

name: app-query
description_raw: Get files from a specific app using glob categories defined in context.globs.json. Use when user says: "get files from X app", "show me X's backend", "load X docs", "what apps have context", "list globs for X", "app files for X", "codebase of X", "understand X app", "X services", "X components", "X frontend", "X api", "X routes", "X tests", "X config", "all RVETS backend code". Requires context.globs.json in the project root (generated by system-context skill).
description_normalized: Get files from a specific app using glob categories defined in context.globs.json.

name: browse-with-me
description_raw: Browser automation where David watches and Claude drives. Pick the right browser tool and operate it correctly. Use when user says "browse with me", "check the site", "open a browser", "use playwright", "playwright mcp", "take a screenshot of", "navigate to", "test the page", "health check", "click through the site", "what does X look like", "is the site working", "check localhost", "open this URL", "agent-browser", "browse to", "look at this page", "verify the UI", "debug console errors", "check my open tabs", "inspect existing tab", "chrome devtools", "what's in the console", "screenshot my browser", "check this file in chrome". David always watches headed browser — never run headless unless explicitly asked.
description_normalized: Browser automation where David watches and Claude drives.

name: create-appystack
description_raw: Create a new AppyStack (RVETS) project from scratch using the create-appystack CLI. RVETS = React 19 + Vite 7 + Express 5 + TypeScript + Socket.io — npm workspaces monorepo with client/, server/, shared/ packages. Trigger on: "create a new AppyStack app", "scaffold a new RVETS project", "new AppyStack project", "create-appystack", "new full-stack app with AppyStack", "spin up an AppyStack". DO NOT invoke if the current working directory is already an AppyStack project — detected by presence of .claude/skills/recipe/ in CWD. Inside an existing AppyStack project, the "recipe" skill handles feature scaffolding instead.
description_normalized: Create a new AppyStack (RVETS) project from scratch using the create-appystack CLI.

name: five-personas
description_raw: Run a decision, problem, or idea through 5 independent cognitive lenses in parallel, then synthesise into a Chairman recommendation. Use when: 'five personas', 'run through the personas', 'stress test this idea', 'get five perspectives', 'challenge this decision', 'what am I missing', 'think about this differently
description_normalized: Run a decision, problem, or idea through 5 independent cognitive lenses in parallel, then synthesise into a Chairman recommendation.

name: goal-plan
description_raw: Interview the user through a structured plan schema (Stack, In Scope, Out of Scope, Definition of Done, Acceptance Criteria, Key References) and produce two artifacts — a plan markdown document and a paste-ready 1-line /goal condition for Claude Code's autonomous goal feature (v2.1.139+, max 4000 chars). Use when the user says "goal plan", "build a plan", "set a goal", "spec this", "plan for goal", "plan for /goal", "make me a plan", "plan this out before we build", "I want to use /goal", or any variation of "build a structured plan before invoking /goal". Supports four interview modes — code, content, research, freeform — selected up front so the questions match the work. Does NOT execute the plan; produces the inputs that /goal then runs.
description_normalized: Interview the user through a structured plan schema (Stack, In Scope, Out of Scope, Definition of Done, Acceptance Criteria, Key References) and produce two artifacts — a plan markdown document and a paste-ready 1-line /goal condition for Claude Code's autonomous goal feature (v2.1.139+, max 4000 chars).

name: mochaccino
description_raw: Mochaccino — UI/UX design system orchestrator. Entry point for all design work. Manages the full design loop: interview → data → views → revisions → gallery. Works with three specialists: Peter (data), Mocha (view renderer), and Shelly (Shape Librarian). Use when anyone says "mochaccino", "mockup", "design a screen", "let's do a mochaccino", "I want to design something", "explore a UI idea", "wireframe this", "what would X look like", "mock up the UI for", "new workspace", "add a workspace", "show me the gallery", "list designs", "create a parallel workspace", "create a version 2", "start a new design session". Never bypassed — always the entry point even when delegating to Peter, Mocha, or Shelly.
description_normalized: Mochaccino — UI/UX design system orchestrator.

name: peter
description_raw: Peter — data specialist for the Mochaccino ecosystem. Manages the Providence data lifecycle: create, refresh, snapshot, fork, and discover JSON data shapes. Part of the Mochaccino trio (with Mochaccino and Shelly). Use when Mochaccino asks Peter to create or update data, or when user says "ask Peter to", "update the data", "refresh the JSON", "create a data shape", "snapshot this", "fork the data", "Peter can you", "what does the data look like". All data files live in .mochaccino/data/ and follow strict schema conventions.
description_normalized: Peter — data specialist for the Mochaccino ecosystem.

name: ralphy
description_raw: Ralphy — David's Ralph Wiggum approach for autonomous batch campaigns on existing projects. Use when user says "ralphy", "run ralphy", "start a ralph campaign", "new campaign", "new PR", "new worktree", "start a wave", "plan this", "build this", "extend this", "add features", "run a coordinator loop", "what does ralphy do", "switch to analysis", "switch to development", "switch to content", "switch profile", or any mention of running an autonomous batch of work (planning, building, extending) on an existing project. Ralphy has four modes: Requirements, Plan, Build, Extend — and three campaign profiles: Development, Analysis, Content. He is directive — he reads current state and tells the user where things are and what's available.
description_normalized: Ralphy — David's Ralph Wiggum approach for autonomous batch campaigns on existing projects.

name: remote-machines
description_raw: Run commands, copy files, or execute workflows on David's other machines via SSH. Use when user says "run this on the mac mini", "run it on the other machine", "ssh to mac-mini", "deploy to the agent machine", "bot machine", "headless machine", "workstation machine", "send this to mac-mini-m4", "mac-mini-m2", "remote machines", "other machines", or any request involving executing something on a machine other than the current one. Also use proactively when a task would clearly benefit from running on a specific machine (e.g. a background job on the headless agent machine).
description_normalized: Run commands, copy files, or execute workflows on David's other machines via SSH.

name: spec-writer
description_raw: Expert product owner, business analyst, and requirements strategist. Takes a long, messy brainstorm and transforms it into a clear, structured, decision-useful requirements document. Use when user says: "spec writer", "write a spec", "turn this brainstorm into a spec", "requirements document", "spec this out", "product spec", "BA this", "write the requirements for", "turn this into a requirements doc", "write the PRD", "document these requirements". Works on any app, domain, or system. Reusable across App 1, App 2, and any other project.
description_normalized: Expert product owner, business analyst, and requirements strategist.

name: ux-writer
description_raw: Expert UX analyst, interaction designer, and experience strategist. Takes a long, messy brainstorm — conversations, session notes, sketches described in words, rough ideas — and transforms it into a clear, structured, decision-useful UX requirements document. Produces screen inventories, user flows, interaction specifications, information architecture, and UX business rules. Use when user says: "ux writer", "write the UX spec", "document the UX", "UX requirements", "interaction spec", "write up the user flows", "document how this works from a UX perspective", "turn this into a UX spec", "what are the screens", "map the user journey", "write the experience requirements". Works on any app, domain, or system.
description_normalized: Expert UX analyst, interaction designer, and experience strategist.

name: refresh-bmad-brain

Triggers on phrases like "refresh bmad brain", "update bmad docs", "bmad updates", "what's new in bmad", "bmad v6 changes", "bmad version 6", "refresh bmad v4", "bmad version 4 updates". Supports version-specific refresh (v4 stable vs v6 alpha).
description_normalized: Use this skill when the user wants to refresh, update, or discover what's new in the BMAD Method second brain documentation.

name: ce-brainstorm

Use when the user says "let's brainstorm", "what should we build", or "help me think through X", presents a vague or ambitious feature request, or seems unsure about scope or direction -- even without explicitly asking to brainstorm.
description_normalized: Explores requirements via collaborative dialogue and produces a right-sized requirements document that answers WHAT to build before planning begins.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-brainstorm
frontmatter_yaml.description: Explore requirements and approaches through collaborative dialogue, then write a right-sized requirements document.

name: ce-doc-review
description_raw: Review requirements or plan documents using parallel persona agents that surface role-specific issues. Use when a requirements document or plan document exists and the user wants to improve it.
description_normalized: Dispatches specialized document-reviewer agents in parallel, auto-applies safe fixes, and routes remaining findings through a four-option interaction for user decision.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-doc-review
frontmatter_yaml.description: Review requirements or plan documents using parallel persona agents that surface role-specific issues.

name: ce-ideate
description_raw: Generate and critically evaluate grounded ideas about a topic. Use when asking what to improve, requesting idea generation, exploring surprising directions, or wanting the AI to proactively suggest strong options before brainstorming one in depth.
description_normalized: Generates and critically evaluates grounded ideas, producing a ranked ideation artifact in docs/ideation/ before brainstorm or planning begins.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-ideate
frontmatter_yaml.description: Generate and critically evaluate grounded ideas about a topic.

name: ce-proof
description_raw: Run human-in-the-loop review loops over markdown via Proof (proofeditor.ai) -- share, view, comment on, edit, and sync collaborative docs. Use when the user says "view this in proof", "share to proof", "HITL this doc", or wants a shared markdown review surface for a spec, plan, or draft.
description_normalized: Shares markdown documents to the Proof collaborative editor for human-in-the-loop review, supporting both Web API and Local Bridge modes.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-proof
frontmatter_yaml.description: Run human-in-the-loop review loops over markdown via Proof (proofeditor.ai).

description_normalized: Turns raw Riffrec screen+voice session recordings into structured product feedback, routing between setup, quick bug report, and extensive analysis paths.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-riffrec-feedback-analysis
frontmatter_yaml.description: Riffrec product-feedback workflow.

name: ce-strategy
description_raw: Create or maintain STRATEGY.md - the product's target problem, approach, users, key metrics, and tracks of work. Use when starting a new product, updating direction, or when prompts like 'write our strategy', 'update the roadmap', 'what are we working on', or 'set up the strategy doc' come up.
description_normalized: Creates and maintains STRATEGY.md as a short durable anchor document capturing product target, approach, users, metrics, and investment tracks.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-strategy
frontmatter_yaml.description: Create or maintain STRATEGY.md.

name: ce-adversarial-document-reviewer
description_raw: Conditional document-review persona for high-stakes documents -- those with significant architectural decisions, new abstractions, or more than 5 requirements. Challenges premises, surfaces unstated assumptions, and stress-tests decisions rather than evaluating document quality.
description_normalized: Conditionally applied document-review agent that challenges plan premises and stress-tests decisions via a 5-technique falsification protocol.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-adversarial-document-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Conditional document-review persona for high-stakes documents.

name: ce-coherence-reviewer
description_raw: Reviews planning documents for internal consistency -- contradictions between sections, terminology drift, structural issues, and ambiguity where readers would diverge. Spawned by the document-review skill.
description_normalized: Document-review agent that catches internal contradictions, terminology drift, and structural inconsistencies within requirements or plan documents.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-coherence-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Reviews planning documents for internal consistency.

name: ce-design-lens-reviewer
description_raw: Reviews planning documents for missing design decisions -- information architecture, interaction states, user flows, and AI slop risk. Uses dimensional rating to identify gaps. Spawned by the document-review skill.
description_normalized: Document-review agent that identifies missing design decisions in plans using a dimensional 0-10 rating system, flagging only scores below 7.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-design-lens-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Reviews planning documents for missing design decisions.

name: ce-feasibility-reviewer
description_raw: Evaluates whether proposed technical approaches in planning documents will survive contact with reality -- architecture conflicts, dependency gaps, migration risks, and implementability. Spawned by the document-review skill.
description_normalized: Document-review agent that evaluates whether a plan's technical approach is actually buildable, checking architecture conflicts, dependency gaps, and environmental assumptions.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-feasibility-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Evaluates technical feasibility of planning documents.

name: ce-product-lens-reviewer
description_raw: Reviews planning documents as a senior product leader -- challenges premise claims, assesses strategic consequences (trajectory, identity, adoption, opportunity cost), and surfaces goal-work misalignment. Spawned by the document-review skill.
description_normalized: Document-review agent that challenges premises and assesses strategic consequences of requirements or plans from a senior product leader perspective.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-product-lens-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Reviews planning documents as a senior product leader.

name: ce-scope-guardian-reviewer
description_raw: Reviews planning documents for scope alignment and unjustified complexity -- challenges unnecessary abstractions, premature frameworks, and scope that exceeds stated goals. Spawned by the document-review skill.
description_normalized: Document-review agent that asks whether plans are right-sized for their goals and whether every abstraction earns its keep.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-scope-guardian-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Reviews planning documents for scope alignment and unjustified complexity.

name: ce-security-lens-reviewer
description_raw: Evaluates planning documents for security gaps at the plan level -- auth/authz assumptions, data exposure risks, API surface vulnerabilities, and missing threat model elements. Spawned by the document-review skill.
description_normalized: Document-review agent that evaluates security gaps in plans before implementation — threat model completeness, auth/authz commitments, and secret handling strategy.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-security-lens-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Evaluates planning documents for security gaps.

name: ce-spec-flow-analyzer
description_raw: Analyzes specifications and feature descriptions for user flow completeness and gap identification. Use when a spec, plan, or feature description needs flow analysis, edge case discovery, or requirements validation.
description_normalized: Analyzes specs and feature descriptions for missing user flows, ambiguous requirements, and edge cases from the end-user perspective before implementation.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-spec-flow-analyzer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Analyzes specifications for user flow completeness and gap identification.

name: accessibility

description_normalized: Design, implement, and audit inclusive digital products using WCAG 2.2 Level AA standards.

frontmatter_yaml.name: accessibility
frontmatter_yaml.description: Design, implement, and audit inclusive digital products using WCAG 2.2 Level AA standards. Use this skill to generate semantic ARIA for Web and accessibility traits for Web and Native platforms (iOS/Android).

name: agent-sort
description_raw: Build an evidence-backed ECC install plan for a specific repo by sorting skills, commands, rules, hooks, and extras into DAILY vs LIBRARY buckets using parallel repo-aware review passes. Use when ECC should be trimmed to what a project actually needs instead of loading the full bundle.

name: token-budget-advisor
description_raw: Offers the user an informed choice about how much response depth to consume before answering. Use this skill when the user explicitly wants to control response length, depth, or token budget. TRIGGER when: "token budget", "token count", "token usage", "token limit", "response length", "answer depth", "short version", "brief answer", "detailed answer", "exhaustive answer", "respuesta corta vs larga", "cuántos tokens", "ahorrar tokens", "responde al 50%", "dame la versión corta", "quiero controlar cuánto usas", or clear variants where the user is explicitly asking to control answer size or depth. DO NOT TRIGGER when: user has already specified a level in the current session (maintain it), the request is clearly a one-word answer, or "token" refers to auth/session/payment tokens rather than response size.
description_normalized: Offers the user an informed choice about how much response depth to consume before answering.

frontmatter_yaml.name: token-budget-advisor
frontmatter_yaml.description: Offers the user an informed choice about how much response depth to consume before answering. Use this skill when the user explicitly wants to control response length, depth, or token budget. TRIGGER when: "token budget", "token count", "token usage", "token limit", "response length", "answer depth", "short version", "brief answer", "detailed answer", "exhaustive answer", "respuesta corta vs larga", "cuántos tokens", "ahorrar tokens", "responde al 50%", "dame la versión corta", "quiero controlar cuánto usas", or clear variants where the user is explicitly asking to control answer size or depth. DO NOT TRIGGER when: user has already specified a level in the current session (maintain it), the request is clearly a one-word answer, or "token" refers to auth/session/payment tokens rather than response size.

description_raw: Initialize a new project with deep context gathering and PROJECT.md. Includes a Copilot runtime note for VS Code compatibility (vscode_askquestions equivalent to AskUserQuestion).
description_normalized: Initializes a new project with deep context gathering, requirements extraction, and PROJECT.md creation.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:new-project
frontmatter_yaml.description: Initialize a new project with deep context gathering and PROJECT.md

name: gsd:ui-phase
description_raw: Generate UI design contract (UI-SPEC.md) for frontend phases. Spawns gsd-ui-researcher then gsd-ui-checker in a verification loop.
description_normalized: Generates a UI-SPEC.md design contract for frontend phases using researcher and checker subagents.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:ui-phase
frontmatter_yaml.description: Generate UI design contract (UI-SPEC.md) for frontend phases

name: gsd-advisor-researcher
description_raw: Researches a single gray area decision and returns a structured comparison table with rationale. Spawned by discuss-phase advisor mode. Returns structured output for the main agent to synthesize — does NOT present to user directly.
description_normalized: Researches a single gray-area decision and returns a structured 5-column comparison table for discuss-phase synthesis.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd-advisor-researcher
frontmatter_yaml.description: Researches a single gray area decision and returns a structured comparison table with rationale. Spawned by discuss-phase advisor mode.
frontmatter_yaml.tools: Read, Bash, Grep, Glob, WebSearch, WebFetch, mcp__context7__*

name: gsd-roadmapper
description_raw: Creates project roadmaps with phase breakdown, requirement mapping, success criteria derivation, and coverage validation. Spawned by /gsd-new-project orchestrator.
description_normalized: Creates project roadmaps mapping requirements to phases with goal-backward success criteria and coverage validation.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd-roadmapper
frontmatter_yaml.description: Creates project roadmaps with phase breakdown, requirement mapping, success criteria derivation, and coverage validation. Spawned by /gsd-new-project orchestrator.

name: gsd-ui-checker
description_raw: Validates UI-SPEC.md design contracts against 6 quality dimensions. Produces BLOCK/FLAG/PASS verdicts. Spawned by /gsd-ui-phase orchestrator.
description_normalized: Validates UI-SPEC.md contracts against 6 quality dimensions, returning BLOCK/FLAG/PASS verdicts.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd-ui-checker
frontmatter_yaml.description: Validates UI-SPEC.md design contracts against 6 quality dimensions. Produces BLOCK/FLAG/PASS verdicts. Spawned by /gsd-ui-phase orchestrator.

name: gsd-ui-researcher
description_raw: Produces UI-SPEC.md design contract for frontend phases. Reads upstream artifacts, detects design system state, asks only unanswered questions. Spawned by /gsd-ui-phase orchestrator.
description_normalized: Produces UI-SPEC.md design contracts for frontend phases by reading upstream artifacts and asking only unanswered design questions.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd-ui-researcher
frontmatter_yaml.description: Produces UI-SPEC.md design contract for frontend phases. Reads upstream artifacts, detects design system state, asks only unanswered questions. Spawned by /gsd-ui-phase orchestrator.

handoffs.label: Build Specification

description_normalized: Surfaces up to 5 clarification questions for an existing spec and encodes the answers back into spec.md.

name: brainstorming
description_raw: You MUST use this before any creative work - creating features, building components, adding functionality, or modifying behavior. Explores user intent, requirements and design before implementation. Guides a structured spec-and-design dialogue: one question at a time, 2-3 approach proposals, design approval gate, write design doc, spec self-review, then hand off to writing-plans.
description_normalized: Structured design dialogue skill that turns ideas into approved spec documents before any implementation begins.

frontmatter_yaml.name: brainstorming
frontmatter_yaml.description: You MUST use this before any creative work - creating features, building components, adding functionality, or modifying behavior. Explores user intent, requirements and design before implementation.
